title: asclepias adscendens
intro: Asclepias adscendens, also known as the climbing milkweed, is a perennial plant species native to the Americas that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to induce vomiting and act as a laxative. The health benefits of Asclepias adscendens include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to protect against certain parasites and infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat a range of health issues due to its various bioactive compounds.
